design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate alien environments, solve environmental puzzles, overcome obstacles through platforming and combat using a multi-mode laser weapon; survive hazards and enemy encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: One-hit lethality combined with environmental hazards and enemy encounters; player must master timing, weapon modes, and spatial awareness to progress
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Survive on an alien world as a scientist thrust into danger; escape captivity and restore control of your fate through cunning and combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately minimal UI and no textual guidance; story told through cinematic presentation and alien language dialogue rather than exposition; forces player agency through environmental discovery and trial-and-error learning; no HUD except oxygen meter; checkpoint-based progression with no hand-holding

difficulty.lives_system: One-hit death system; Lester and ally cannot sustain any damage; struck by projectiles, contact with animals, or environmental hazards ends the game immediately
difficulty.continues_system: Unlimited checkpoint restarts; game ends on damage but player restarts at last checkpoint indefinitely
difficulty.checkpointing: Numerous checkpoints throughout levels; alphanumeric codes allow players to manually record checkpoint positions for later re-entry on systems without save functionality
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High environmental interaction required; player must discover weapon capabilities (standard fire, force field, charged shot) and learn enemy patterns through repeated attempts; puzzle-solving bypasses some combat encounters

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon acquisition progression: Lester begins unarmed (can only kick small creatures); acquires laser pistol from fallen enemy mid-game; pistol gradually reveals three capabilities (standard fire, force field generation, charged shots)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Environmental progression through prison complex, cave system, and tower structure; weapon modes unlocked implicitly through discovery and enemy encounters rather than explicit tutorials
replayability_hooks: Multiple laser weapon modes encourage experimentation with different solutions to same encounters
Environmental hazards require learning precise timing patterns
Puzzle variety prevents pure memorization solutions
Alien ally 'Buddy' creates cooperative puzzle-solving moments

world_building_techniques: Opening cinematic establishes premise: physicist Lester conducting particle accelerator experiment struck by lightning, causing teleportation to alien world
Cinematic visual storytelling with minimal dialogue; aliens speak in unintelligible alien language
Environmental progression (prison camp → cave system → tower) reveals world hierarchy and stakes
Character relationship development through gameplay: alien captive 'Buddy' becomes ally, culminating in rescue sequence
Climactic narrative beat: Lester wounded in final encounter, rescued by Buddy who carries him to escape vessel (dragon-like creature)
No on-screen text or UI narration; story conveyed entirely through animation, environment design, and cutscenes

boss_design.philosophy: Integrated into narrative climax rather than discrete boss encounters; final alien antagonist serves both combat challenge and story resolution; previous encounters are environmental hazards and enemy formations rather than named bosses
notable_bosses: Final alien antagonist: severely wounds Lester before being defeated with Buddy's assistance; represents the primary antagonistic force of the alien captors

faction_indicators: Humanoid alien race operating prison camp (captors/antagonists)
Buddy (alien ally faction) - prisoner like Lester, represents potential for cross-species cooperation
danger_signals: Alien soldier presence and active pursuit of escaped prisoners
Environmental hazards throughout terrain (lethal contact with most obstacles)
Lester's complete vulnerability without weapon (unarmed initial state)
Oxygen depletion during underwater sequences (oxygen bar provides only mechanic-specific UI)
environmental_storytelling: Prison camp design communicates oppressive alien civilization with advanced technology
Underground laboratory vs. alien world contrast establishes scale of dimensional shift
Tower structure as final escape route suggests hierarchical alien settlement
Dragon-like creature available at climax implies alien fauna domestication
Fallen enemy weapon pickup establishes combat possibility and alien technology parity
